chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No.698 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATUREs OF JUDGEs 1. For orders on M.A. No.4120/2025 (U/A) 2. For orders on M.A. No.4121/2025 (Exp/A) 3. For hearing of bail application ------------------------- 17.03.2025 Mr. Abdul Aleem Kalhoro, advocate for applicant ------------------------------------ Applicant/accused Ehsan Ali son of Subhan Ali and Subhan Ali son of Muhammad Siddiq seeks pre-arrest bail in FIR No.79/2025, registered at P.S. “A” Section Ghotki for offence under secions 452, 365-B, PPC read with Section 3 of the Prevention of Trafficking in Persons Act, 2018. Learned counsel for applicants submits that applicants are innocent and they has been falsely implicated in this case due to mala fide intentions and ulterior motives; that applicants/accused are law abiding citizens and they intend to surrender before the concerned Court but they feel apprehension of their arrest at the hands of police, hence they need some protection to enable them to appear before the Court concerned. Without touching the merits of the case, protective bail is granted to applicants/accused above named, for a period of five days from today, subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.25,000/- (Rupees twenty five thousand) each and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. Applicants/accused are directed to surrender before the trial Court concerned in the aforesaid period and join the investigation/trial forthwith. This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of five days from today or whenever applicants/accused surrender before the trial Court, which one occurs earlier.

Applicants/accused are directed to surrender before the trial Court concerned in the aforesaid period and join the investigation/trial forthwith. This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of five days from today or whenever applicants/accused surrender before the trial Court, which one occurs earlier. It is made clear, in case, applicants/accused fail to surrender before the trial Court in the aforesaid period, their bail bonds shall stand automatically forfeited. The instant criminal bail application is accordingly disposed of. J U D G E Gulsher/PS
